title: Lesson 4 Quiz: Subtracting Two Mixed Numbers
description: Subtract mixed numbers with unlike denominators and regrouping.

questions.hint: Find the LCD of 3 and 5 (it's 15), then convert both fractions before subtracting. No borrowing needed here since 2/3 is bigger than 2/5 once converted.
questions.explanation: LCD is 15. 2/3=10/15, 2/5=6/15. Fraction diff is 4/15 and whole diff is 4.

questions.hint: Convert 1/4 into eighths so it matches the denominator of 6/8, then subtract the fraction parts directly.
The LCD of 10 and 6 is 30. Convert both fractions first, then check that 9/10 (as 27/30) is still bigger than 2/6 (as 10/30) so no borrowing is needed.
questions.explanation: Use LCD 30: 9/10=27/30, 2/6=10/30. Fraction difference is 17/30; whole difference is 1.

questions.hint: Convert 1/2 and 1/3 to sixths. Since 3/6 is bigger than 2/6, you can subtract directly without borrowing.
The LCD of 8 and 3 is 24. Convert both fractions, then subtract since 5/8 is already larger than 1/3.
questions.explanation: LCD 24: 5/8=15/24 and 1/3=8/24. Fraction difference is 7/24; whole is 14.

questions.hint: Convert 3/5 and 3/4 to twentieths. Since 12/20 is smaller than 15/20, you'll need to borrow 1 from the whole number 17 before subtracting.
Since 1/2 is smaller than 3/4, you can't subtract directly. Borrow 1 from the whole number 25 and rewrite it as a fraction with the same denominator as 3/4.
Convert 1/2 to sixths to match 5/6. Since 3/6 is smaller than 5/6, borrow 1 from 87 before subtracting.
questions.explanation: LCD 6: 1/2=3/6. Borrow 1 from 87 gives 86 and 9/6. 9/6-5/6=4/6=2/3, whole diff 31.

questions.hint: Convert 1/3 and 1/2 to sixths. Since 2/6 is smaller than 3/6, borrow 1 from the whole number 18 before subtracting.
questions.explanation: LCD 6: 1/3=2/6, 1/2=3/6. Borrow 1 gives 17 and 8/6. 8/6-3/6=5/6. Whole difference 2.

questions.hint: Convert 1/3 and 3/4 to twelfths. Since 4/12 is smaller than 9/12, borrow 1 from 24 before subtracting.
Find the LCD of 8 and 3 (it's 24), convert both fractions, and subtract since 5/8 is already larger than 1/3.
questions.explanation: LCD 24: 5/8=15/24 and 1/3=8/24. Fraction difference 7/24, whole difference 1.

questions.hint: Convert 2/5 and 1/4 to twentieths. Since 8/20 is bigger than 5/20, you can subtract without needing to borrow.
questions.explanation: LCD 20: 2/5=8/20 and 1/4=5/20. Difference is 3/20 with whole difference 9.

questions.hint: Simplify 6/10 to 3/5 first, then convert both fractions to twentieths. Since 12/20 is smaller than 15/20, you'll need to borrow from the whole number before subtracting.
Convert 1/2 and 2/3 to sixths. Since 3/6 is smaller than 4/6, borrow 1 from the whole number 3 before subtracting.
questions.explanation: LCD 6: 1/2=3/6, 2/3=4/6. Borrow 1 from 3 gives 2 and 9/6. 9/6-4/6=5/6; whole 1.

questions.hint: Convert 1/2 and 1/3 to sixths. Since 3/6 is already bigger than 2/6, you can subtract directly — no borrowing needed.
questions.explanation: Convert to sixths: 1/2=3/6 and 1/3=2/6. Difference is 1 and 1/6.
